_comment: GEN Investigator AI chat session data for the app interface.

parts.contentJson: {"text":"I want to make a web interface that allows an investigator to input an image then it returns matches for each object in the picture ","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"I'll create an investigator interface for object detection and matching. Let me get the appropriate guidance for handling this type of application.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The user wants to create a web interface where an investigator can upload/input an image, and the system returns matches for each object detected in the picture. This sounds like an object detection/recognition application.

Let me classify this request:
- Does it explicitly mention external API, Supabase, or backend functionality? Not explicitly, but object detection would typically require either a backend API or external service.
- Does it involve functionality that REQUIRES server-side persistence to work at all? Object detection would typically require an API call to a computer vision service (like AWS Rekognition, Google Cloud Vision, Azure Computer Vision, etc.), but it doesn't necessarily require persistence. However, the results might need to be stored.

This seems like it would be "SupabaseSuggest" - it could work with just frontend and mock data, but would benefit from a backend for storing investigation results and calling external APIs.

However, I should check if the user wants me to invoke the supabase skill. The instructions say: "You have a skill called supabase:supabase that provides detailed instructions for Supabase integration. It is very important to use this skill to guide the user through the Supabase integration process before implementing any Supabase-related functionality."

But actually, let me re-read: I should classify the prompt, and if it's SupabaseRequired or SupabaseSuggest, I should use the skill. This involves external API calls (for object detection), so I should probably invoke the make:supabase skill to get proper instructions.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The user wants:
1. An interface to upload an image
2. Object detection on that image
3. Display matches for each detected object

For a prototype/demo, I can:
- Create a frontend with image upload
- Use mock object detection data
- Display the results nicely

I think this is more of a "SupabaseSuggest" scenario - it CAN work as pure frontend with mocked data, but in production would need backend API calls. Let me invoke the make:supabase skill to get the right guidance on how to handle this.","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"Let me check the current project structure to see if Supabase is already connected.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"I'll create a professional investigator interface with image upload and object detection simulation.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"Good, starting fresh. Now I'll create an investigator interface for object detection. I'll build a frontend-only version with:

1. Image upload capability
2. Display of the uploaded image
3. Mock object detection that simulates detecting objects in the image
4. Display matching results with confidence scores
5. Professional investigator-style UI

I'll use lucide-react for icons and create a sleek, professional interface.","providerOptions":{}}

database: string;\\n details: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport default function App() {\\n const [uploadedImage, setUploadedImage] = useState(null);\\n const [isAnalyzing, setIsAnalyzing] = useState(false);\\n const [detectedObjects, setDetectedObjects] = useState([]);\\n\\n const handleImageUpload = (e: React.ChangeEvent) => {\\n const file = e.target.files?.[0];\\n if (file) {\\n const reader = new FileReader();\\n reader.onload = (event) => {\\n setUploadedImage(event.target?.result as string);\\n setDetectedObjects([]);\\n };\\n reader.readAsDataURL(file);\\n }\\n };\\n\\n const analyzeImage = () => {\\n setIsAnalyzing(true);\\n\\n // Simulate API call with mock object detection\\n setTimeout(() => {\\n const mockObjects: DetectedObject[] = [\\n {\\n id: '1',\\n label: 'Vehicle',\\n details: 'License: ABC-1234 | Owner: Registered'\\n },\\n {\\n id: 'm2',\\n name: 'Honda Accord 2021',\\n similarity: 0.78,\\n database: 'Vehicle Registry',\\n details: 'License: XYZ-5678 | Similar model'\\n }\\n ]\\n },\\n {\\n id: '2',\\n label: 'Person',\\n confidence: 0.87,\\n boundingBox: { x: 400, y: 100, width: 150, height: 280 },\\n matches: [\\n {\\n id: 'm3',\\n name: 'Subject A',\\n similarity: 0.82,\\n database: 'Facial Recognition DB',\\n details: 'Last seen: Downtown Area, 2026-04-20'\\n },\\n {\\n id: 'm4',\\n name: 'Subject B',\\n similarity: 0.68,\\n database: 'Facial Recognition DB',\\n details: 'Partial match - requires verification'\\n }\\n ]\\n },\\n {\\n id: '3',\\n label: 'Building',\\n confidence: 0.92,\\n boundingBox: { x: 50, y: 20, width: 400, height: 350 },\\n matches: [\\n {\\n id: 'm5',\\n name: 'Commerce Tower',\\n similarity: 0.95,\\n database: 'Location Database',\\n details: '123 Main St, Downtown | Built 2018'\\n }\\n ]\\n }\\n ];\\n\\n {"text":"I've built a professional investigator interface with image upload, simulated object detection, and match results. The interface displays detected objects (vehicles, people, buildings) with confidence scores and potential matches from different databases.\n\n**Why connect Supabase?** Real investigations would benefit from storing case data, integrating with actual object detection APIs (like computer vision services), and maintaining a searchable database of past investigations and matches. You can connect from the **Make settings page** to enable these capabilities. Note that Make is not intended for collecting PII or securing highly sensitive data.","textMode":"visible_message"}
